creator: lcook@freebsd.org
text:
Hi, thank you for the submission. Just a few things:

- When generating a patch, it's best to `svn diff` against the port(s) tree as oppose to a unified diff. This keeps the paths/relevant meta-data in check and makes committing _much_ easier. For further information see here: https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/port-upgrading.html 
- Attaching relevant logs (.e.g. Poudriere build/testport logs) leaves less room for interpretation and puts everyone on the same page. It be worth looking into Poudriere, see here: https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/testing-poudriere.html
 
^Triage: [tags] in issue Titles are deprecated
